entry.title: Re: Exploring Integration Options available in SAP Master Data Governance on S/4HANA
entry.content:
Dear Vijay,

We have a requirement to integrate MDG s4hana 2023 on-premise with s4 Hana public cloud 2402, so I am thinking of using DRF with web services. What would you recommend in this case?

 

Regards

Rahul Khandelwal

entry.title: Re: How to set an default value into VL03n->HeaderInformation->Conditions ?
entry.content:
Hello @shiz0frenik 

Looks like you have customized ZF00 condition type but you are missing the condition type determination settings. A condition is automatically determined with Condition Technique 

However the catch is that your condition type is a header level one. As per SAP Help on Header and Item Conditions:

Header Conditions
Automatic pricing does not take header conditions into account; you can not create condition records for them in the standard system. Header conditions are entered manually in order processing.

So you want a condition type to be determined automatically you need to defined it as an item level condition.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: April (Citizen) Developer Challenge – SAP Build Apps: Task 2 - Formulas
entry.content:
Hash: 8da8848f262caf6be3aaf0afeba809bd87b2821567bd156a841a32a51fc6f42d

Very useful challenge to get familiar with Formulas!

entry.title: Re: Is there any T-code for dynamic report for single and...
entry.content: On S/4 HANA look at transaction SE16H

entry.title: Re: Quote 2.0: Post requests of a Rest Client
entry.content:
Hi Cyrine,

It seems like you are attempting to include error messages in the event of a failed API call. If this is the case, it is likely that the scenario will occur when the RestClient.Post execution encounters errors such as HTTP 400/500 errors. In order to handle these potential errors, you will need to use a "try" command to catch them. Otherwise, the script will halt execution when an error occurs.

Sample syntax: 

try:
    response = RestClient.Post(url, data, headers)

except Exception as e:
    context.Quote.AddMessage("message text", MessageLevel.Error, True)

When it comes to "loop" logic, you will need to incorporate "if" logic within a "for" loop.

Best Regards

Fancy

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Application Studio: Can't see entity whe...
entry.content: In the searchbar, search for ">open storyboard" and proceed from there 🙂

entry.title: Re: Use Data Export Service to Replicate Data Between SAP Analytics Cloud Tenants
entry.content:
Hi @AmandaJMurphy 

Thanks for your response. Yes, the error is triggered when the hamburger icon is selected. And yes, we are able to manually enter values in the fields (such as public.RF1) and this does filter the export as expected, so we have been using this as a workaround. But it would be easier to be able to select values using a member selector, e.g. if there are many values to select, or if the ID is not known, or if we wanted to select based on hierarchy parent.

Is there any known solution for this error? 

Thanks,

Hugh

entry.title: Client certificate based Authorization in AS2 adapter?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I had a requirement, we have multiple sender into SAP CPI but we have one connectivity, We are able to run the scenario with Standard authorization as user role ( & the user role is ESBMessaging.send ).

 

But now, we have to use the client certificate as authorization. Please suggest how to achieve this?

Thanks in Advance!

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano,

This initiative sounds fantastic, and I'm definitely interested in learning more about SAP.

My name is Adrian. While I don't necessarily fall under the listed categories (though as a recently transitioned consultant due to restructuring, I understand the value of career pivots!), I have a strong passion for technology and a proven track record of learning new skills quickly.

I'm currently diving into the "Exploring the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Implementation Fundamentals" path and eager to see where it takes me. If there's any way I can participate in the digital skills initiative, even if in a slightly different capacity, I'd love to hear more!

 

Looking forward to your reply,

Best regards,

Adrian

entry.title: Re: UI5 bank account
entry.content: The UI5 component was not loaded for the intent "#BankAccount- manageMasterData"

entry.title: Re: Tips about default search help at Collective Search Helps
entry.content:
@raphlma 

This post doesn't talk about assignment of a search help to screen field, but about the default elementary search help which is being displayed for a user in a collective search help.

entry.title: Re: The last purchase price of the material
entry.content:
Hello @T_Vasanthakumar 

The ME1P transaction gives a nice overview of purchasing price history.

Any info record report like ME1L, ME1M, ME1W provide the following fields:

  • price origin - "Pur. Order" means that the price comes from the last purchase order
  • net price - the price from the last purchase order
  • Pur. Doc / Item - the last purchase order and item numbers
  • Date - the date of the last purchase order

The fields are visible in the standard layout of those transactions.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: How to dynamically set the metaPath in an xml fragment?
entry.content: Ah, well that's a little disappointing. Thank you for letting me know!

entry.title: SAP BTP and Third-Party Cookies Deprecation
entry.content:
Are You Affected by the Third-Party Cookie Deprecation?

As you may already be aware, browser manufacturers are ending support for third-party cookies. A prominent example is Google Chrome’s deprecation of third-party cookies. We are working to ensure that your SAP solutions continue to function with no or minimal potential change in user experience and your application code. The good news is that now is the perfect time to plan for any future changes you need to make once third-party cookies are deprecated. 

Web applications can embed other applications in iFrames. This architecture enables the user to enjoy a single experience even though they are accessing two different applications, possibly running under different domains. Currently, this architecture relies on third-party cookies to function. The changes proposed by Google for Chrome can potentially break this architecture. If your solutions are not intended to run embedded in iFrames or if or they all run on ondemand.com, or sapcloud.cn, you aren’t affected by this change. 

With this blog, we want to make you aware of where we think you should be testing. Other blogs in this blog series will explain how you can test, how to mitigate any problems you find, and finally adopt a solution based on CHIPS or the Storage Access API, for example. 

 

Integrating Content into SAP Build Work Zone 

SAP Build Work Zone (both standard edition and advanced edition) is a central entry point solution for BTP responsible for integrating content. Applications that integrate into such a central entry point are likely to be affected by the deprecation of third-party cookies. Besides SAP Build Work Zone, other entry points might be affected, for example: 

  • SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone 
  • SAP Cloud Portal Service, Neo Environment 
  • SAP Cloud Portal Service, Cloud Foundry Environment 

 

Fig 1. Content Providers for SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition 

 

Figure 1 shows the typical content providers supported by SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition. See Integrating Business Content in the documentation. 

Content integrated with SAP BTP content providers is not affected by this change. We have taken steps to ensure this content continues to function. 

For content from remote content providers or from manual integrations you might need to take action, as outlined in the following. If the content you integrate doesn’t run on ondemand.com, or sapcloud.cn, we recommend you test your solution according to the testing guide that we will be providing shortly. If you encounter any issues, you may need to request an exception from Google for anything running under your custom domains. We, SAP, have made sure to secure exceptions for the most common central entry points hosted on SAP-owned domains: ondemand.com, cloud.sap and sapcloud.cn. Your application can continue to be integrated into those entry points without any need for modification until the exceptions expire on December 27th, 2024. However, we recommend testing the behavior of your application as outlined in the upcoming post in this blog series to prepare for potentially required change after this date. 

If your application is integrated into entry points running on other domains, you need to act now and manually adjust your application to make use of the exceptions. We will give you more details in an upcoming guide on deprecation trial handling. 

These exceptions keep your solutions running until a solution that supports CHIPS or Storage Access API is implemented before December 27th, 2024. We will provide details on how to adapt your application in a future blog post. 

 

Integrating Content Outside of SAP BTP

Some more advanced scenarios also require testing. If you are integrated into another application outside of SAP BTP, or if you are reusing SAP BTP content outside of SAP BTP to consume it on different domains, make sure to check whether the integration involves iFrames or cookies . If yes, then your application is likely affected by the third-party cookie deprecation. 

 

Further Information

We have created an SAP Note where we listed our solutions: SAP Note 3409306 - Removal of Third-Party Cookies in Google Chrome and Microsoft Edge Browser. Follow this note to stay up to date. 

In a series of upcoming blog posts, we will cover the following topics: 

  • How to test your solution for breakage.
  • How to handle deprecation trial tokens.
  • How to prepare your application for the blockage of third-party cookies.

Please watch this space to stay up to date.

entry.title: UDM_SPECIALIST Mass invoice reason code upload
entry.content:
Hi everyone, 

I am using UDM_SPECIALIST and whenever I get invoice level update from customer, i update the reason code to the selected transaction/ invoice. For example, if customer stated that an invoice is pending due to PO issues, I have to update the reason code as " Po Related " so that our management can see how much uncollected amounts are under perticular issue. In order to upload the reason code, I have to open each account and select the transaction but My question is, is there a way to mass upload the reason code in udm_specialist ?

entry.title: Re: Quote 2.0: Post requests of a Rest Client
entry.content:
Hi Fancy,

The command context.Quote.AddMessage("Message text", MessageLevel.Error, True) is not considered as an error handling command , it should be actually executed after the sucessful call of the API to inform the user that the post request was sucessful. That's why i am not getting why the post request is blocking any further commands.

Best regards

Cyrine

entry.title: What is salv_de_layout_restriction and disvariant-variant?
entry.content:
Hello and good morning!
I'm very much a beginner when it comes to ABAP, and right now I was given the task to go through some ALV code and see how they do it. There's many things I don't understand, one of them being what salv_de_layout_restriction is? In the code it is used as a type, like this:

 

 

types: begin of g_type_s_test,
         amount    type i,
         repid     type syrepid,
         display   type i,
         restrict  type salv_de_layout_restriction,
         default   type sap_bool,
         layout    type disvariant-variant,
         load_layo type sap_bool,
       end of g_type_s_test.

 

 

Maybe I'm being way too detailed when it comes to understanding every little thing, but I really want to know what it is I'm using. I've seen salv_de_layout_restriction be used in other code examples, but haven't found any explanation as to what it is, what you can do with it, etc. So I was wondering if anyone could help me?

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps Preview Issue
entry.content: Good day. I am busy with Learning Journey "Creating a First Application With SAP Build Apps". When previewing the App & scanning the QR code, it does not display the QR code, but rather a Web link to "https://preview.appgyver.com?region=appgyver.com&login=2f...". Upon clicking on OK...nothing happens...it does not show the Numerical QR code as per training material. Pls assist.

entry.title: Re: Connection Error
entry.content:
Hi ramco1917,

Please create SAP Business One related questions under SAP Community → Products and Technology → Enterprise Resource Planning → ERP Q&A category.

About your question, I do not see any SLDServer or LicenseServer property defined in your DI code.

I would suggest you to use SLDServer and test the issue again.

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: BP master data show active, but the list of BP and qu...
entry.content:
This is an old bug from SAP. Unfortunately there are no plans to resolve this issue. The correct field for the queries is frozenFor and not validFor.

SAP note on this:

https://me.sap.com/notes/1569271

Kind regards!

Biser

entry.title: Re: CDP-23559 - Why only for Development Goals?
entry.content:
We want to evaluate the business need for this first as it complicates the system. We do not want to rush into something that is not required for our customers. So is the ask here the same for performance goals?

Also due to capacity this was not picked up in 1H 2024.

entry.title: Re: hey team, can i know how to see the security events l...
entry.content:
The ABAP type STRING can be any length, so it should not be a problem to not know the limit. I guess you may consider the length limit of the fields as defined by RSAUENTR2, but I don't know if it's true for all the fields (it is at least true for user, program name, transaction code, client). Thanks for the feedback about my error concerning the variable message data.

Concerning the variable message data length (e.g. 0131), you're correct, it's what I tried to explain. Anyway, this length is useless to decode the file.

entry.title: Getting error while configuring SAP system in VSCode
entry.content:
Hello SAP experts,

I'm trying to configure SAP system to VScode for the first time, i have go through with multiple blog post but unable to do it. getting below error while configuring it in VScode. please help me out as this issue is pending from last 2 month.

When we check with using link getting this error

1. Error during processing of system information: Error: connect ECONNREFUSED 182.168.3.40:443 

When we check with Ip address getting this error

2. The system URL is using a self-signed security certificate.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Can we develop an Ashynchronus API in the S/4 Hana On-Premises System?
entry.content: It's a very short and incomplete description, so difficult to answer precisely. I guess the answer is probably yes.

entry.title: Re: Important Update: Changes to the Free Tier Option for SAP BTP ABAP Environment
entry.content:
Remember, there is no such thing as "on-premise" in real English.
"Premise" is a concept.
"Premises" is a location.

"https://collectivecontent.agency/2018/04/19/should-i-say-on-premise-or-on-premises-it/"

entry.title: Re: Can't change binding for visibility in ui designer
entry.content:
Hi Koliosis,

If the field configuration property is bound to a standard screen via Extension explorer, I would suggest to delete the CT and re-add it.

If it is all related to a PDI custom screen, I would suggest to update metadata and save and activate the screen.

If it is not work, please raise an incident to SAP with details.

 

Best Regards,

William

entry.title: Re: What is salv_de_layout_restriction and disvariant-variant?
entry.content: If you don't find usage of restrict elsewhere in the code, then it's not used. No need to spend time on it.

entry.title: SAP CDS hierarchy View
entry.content:
Hi experts:

I want use parent field in hierarchy View,but I get this error :

This is my code :

 

define hierarchy ZDDl_PP003B
  with parameters
    P_MATNR : abap.char( 40 )
  as parent child hierarchy(
    source ZDDL_PP003A
    child to parent association _PARENT
    start where
          matnr = :P_MATNR
    siblings order by
      matnr
    multiple parents allowed  )
{
$node.hierarchy_level as levels,
  werks,
  stlan,
  stlal,
  matnr,
  bmeng,
  bmein,
  datuv,
  valid_to,
  idnrk,
  menge * _PARENT.bmeng as menge,
  meins,
  _PARENT

}

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: What is salv_de_layout_restriction and disvariant-variant?
entry.content:
The domain values explain it all:

It defines if layouts can be stored as user specific or cross user ...

Disvariant-variant is one layout variant. A layout variant is a saved  "how the ALV-list looks currently".

entry.title: Best Practices for Configuring Address Fields in Candidate Profile in Recruiting Module
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I'm currently configuring address fields in the Candidate Profile for the Recruiting module and I'm seeking advice on best practices. Is there a way to customize address fields based on the selected country, similar to the Business Configuration rules in Employee Central? For instance, when a candidate selects Taiwan as their country, only the City field should be visible, whereas when they select the U.S.A. or other relevant countries, the State field should also be visible.

In the Candidate Profile, it appears that all address-related fields are visible regardless of the selected country. Currently, I'm only able to use the parent and child pick list to manage field values, rather than field visibility. Ideally, I'd like to ensure that the address collected in the Candidate Profile can seamlessly integrate with Employee Central for hired employees. Alternatively, if necessary, we can prompt the ready-to-hire employee to update their address during the Onboarding process.

Any insights or recommendations on optimizing address field configuration for the Candidate Profile would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: What configuration needs to be done either in ECC(S4) or EWM side to create ID in EWM, skipping IDN?
entry.content:
Hello, 

What configuration needs to be done either in ECC(S4) or EWM side to create Inbound delivery directly in EWM, skipping the need for Inbound delivery notification document?

 

Regards

Bharath

entry.title: Re: Quote 2.0: Post requests of a Rest Client
entry.content: Hi, please incorporate "if" logic within a "for" loop. "if" is only a criteria judgement, not a loop.

entry.title: Re: Setup SAP Task Center with SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @harjeetjudge , thank you for the detailed blog on task center integration with S4 HANA cloud.
I followed the same steps but I am facing 2 issues.
1. When I try to open any item and see the details, I am asked to re-login to my S4 hana cloud system to view the details. I am using the same IAS to open the workzone which I have used for S4 cloud system. Is this a normal behavior to ask for credentials? 

 

2. After I login, the details screen is disabled and I am unable to click on any tab to view more details. The cursor is disabled. Do I need to enable anything from S/4 cloud system?

entry.title: Re: Event mesh error condition = amqp:not-allowed
entry.content:
Hi Abhisek,

I'm also facing the same issue in SAP CPI AMQP receiver adapter.

org.springframework.jms.UncategorizedJmsException: Uncategorized exception occurred during JMS processing; nested exception is javax.jms.JMSException: publishing target (topic:sap/pntdemo/em1/scpi_cts_out) forbidden (220056) [condition = amqp:not-allowed], cause: org.apache.qpid.jms.provider.ProviderException: publishing target (topic:sap/pntdemo/em1/scpi_cts_out) forbidden (220056) [condition = amqp:not-allowed]

Did you find any resolution for this.

Regards,

Suganya

entry.title: Re: Quote 2.0 : Displaying a message to the User in a spe...
entry.content: Hi, you'll need to set the third parameter as "False". Otherwise, once the page is refreshed, the message will be deleted.

entry.title: Re: Integrate SAP Task Center with SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @dhruba_sap  , thank you for the detailed blog on task center integration with S4 HANA cloud.
I followed the same steps but I am facing 2 issues.
1. When I try to open any item and see the details, I am asked to re-login to my S4 hana cloud system to view the details. I am using the same IAS to open the workzone which I have used for S4 cloud system. Is this a normal behavior to ask for credentials? 

 

 

2. After I login, the details screen is disabled and I am unable to click on any tab to view more details. The cursor is disabled. Do I need to enable anything from S/4 cloud system?

entry.title: Re: What is salv_de_layout_restriction and disvariant-variant?
entry.content: Ah thank you, I honestly didn't think to check what's in the domain and I'll remember that for next time. Have a great day!

entry.title: Re: In SAP B1, is it possible to run Depreciation run job via DI API or Service Layer?
entry.content: Hi, thank you for your response. I will review this and check if this resolves our problem. But essentially, if we don't have access to the Depreciation run, we are reverse engineering what it does.

entry.title: Re: Getting error while configuring SAP system in VSCode
entry.content:
Did you already clicked on the link that appears bellow the error message? It seems that have the instructions on how to deal it the error.

But I suspect that will tell you to use a browser to get a copy of the mentioned self signed certificate and import it on your local computer, in order to let your local system recognize it as trusted.

Example: https://support.kaspersky.com/cyber-trace/4.3/174127

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: Great to have you here @JoseDAmico - take a look at Products and Technology boards for all the great blog posts there, as well as Q&A, and "subscribe" to SAP Managed Tags for your topic areas of interest. Let us know any time you have questions.

Regards,
Projit

Access to Intelligent Selling Services

Tenant Provisioning

Intelligent Selling Services

Intelligent Selling Services for SAP Commerce Cloud

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to create a report in SAP SIGNAVIO Exp...
entry.content:
Hi Matthias7,

A custom report can be built outside Signavio which can extract any information from the Signavio Process Manager module using the native API.

However, if you are looking for something simpler which is already available, the "Process Model Metrics" report does extract the folder information for each model. When you run the report,  you can select individual models or the entire folder for the report to look at. The outcome excel then contains the folder names (and breadcrumb path) for each model in column O. 

Hope this helps. 
Projit

entry.title: Re: How to add an icon in Fiori Elements List Report?
entry.content:
Hello @Jocelyn_Dart.

Have we got any updates about this topic? I believe that it is a good feature to have on the Fiori Elements. I have a similar requirement to add ICONS in a column table and even trying a custom column it does not work as expected.

entry.title: log4j vulnerability for APM introscope
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have APM introscope installed on the server and below log4j vulnerabilities found in the scan.

1) /usr/sap/ccms/apmintroscope/APMSqlserver/repo/log4j-1.2.17.jar
2) /usr/sap/ccms/apmintroscope/com.wily.apm.tess/WebContent/WEB-INF/lib/log4j-1.2.9.jar
3) /usr/sap/ccms/apmintroscope/product/enterprisemanager/configuration/org.eclipse.osgi/bundles/133/1/.cp/log4j-1.2.8.jar

We have gone through below documents, but it is not giving exact details.
1) SAP Note - 3130900 - (CVE-2021-44228) Impact of log4j vulnerability to CA Wily Introscope EM Server - Solution Manager - Focused Run
2) Could you please provide us details to fix above log4j vulnerabilities.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: log4j vulnerability for APM introscope
entry.content:
As mentioned in the KBA you share:

APM Engineering has confirmed that APM 9.7 and later servers (Collectors / MOMs / TESS / TIM / WebView / Workstation) and 9.7 (and later)/SaaS/21.x/22.x agents are not vulnerable to CVE-2021-44228

Introscope is a third party tool not managed by SAP so I advise that you bookmark this page on their site:

Introscope - False Positives

It highlights known false positives for Introscope. In regard to the log4j CVE(s), the reason Introscope is not vulnerable is:

Vulnerability Description: JMSAppender in Log4j 1.2 is vulnerable to deserialization of untrusted data when the attacker has write access to the Log4j configuration. The attacker can provide TopicBindingName and TopicConnectionFactoryBindingName configurations causing JMSAppender to perform JNDI requests that result in remote code execution in a similar fashion to CVE-2021-44228. Note this issue only affects Log4j 1.2 when specifically configured to use JMSAppender, which is not the default. Apache Log4j 1.2 reached end of life in August 2015. Users should upgrade to Log4j 2 as it addresses numerous other issues from the previous versions.

The CVE description contains "Note this issue only affects Log4j 1.2 when specifically configured to use JMSAppender, which is not the default."
APM does not configure log4j to use JMS.

It is the same for CVE-2021-44228

As a final recommendation, I suggest that you ensure you are using the latest of introscope for SAP (version 10.8). Broadcom are beginning to remove log4j and using another component called logback:

3247270 - Introscope 10.8 Release Notes

entry.title: Re: Managing exclusions when publishing back to Employee Central
entry.content:
Hi together, 

great post!

we are facing an issue, when users have no change in recurring pay components, but have awarded spot bonus in the compensation process and there was a change in the recurring pay components during the process in EC. 

The publish then overwrites the change of the recurring pay components.

Has anybody a solution for this?

Best regards

Romina

entry.title: Re: April (Citizen) Developer Challenge – SAP Build Apps: Task 2 - Formulas
entry.content: 1f9586d3056af3466272fe3bb5c7fa035fe6f37676b090ed6363e049dc47360f

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano 

Thank you for this incredible opportunity.

In a world where opportunities often seem distant, I stand as a testament to the power of resilience and determination. As a woman, a mother, an immigrant and a person looking to switch their careers mid-life I have traversed various challenges, each one shaping me into the person I am today.

As an immigrant, I left behind the familiarity of my homeland in search of a better, safer life for myself and my kids. The path was arduous, fraught with obstacles and moments of doubt. Yet, with each step forward, I reaffirmed my commitment to forging a brighter future, one built on the foundations of hard work and perseverance.

Now, as I contemplate my career transition - becoming an ABAP developer - I am filled with both excitement and trepidation. I embrace this opportunity for growth, to expand my skill set and explore new avenues of possibility.

The SAP certification program offers a chance to acquire valuable skills that can open doors to a world of opportunities. For someone like me, this opportunity is nothing short of a godsend. It represents not just a chance to enhance my employability but also a means to provide a better future for my family.

To be selected for this program would be an amazing opportunity, a validation of my efforts and a testament to the power of perseverance. It would signify more than just a professional milestone; it would be another symbol of hope, a beacon lighting the way forward.

Thanks and regards!

entry.title: Re: April (Citizen) Developer Challenge – SAP Build Apps: Task 2 - Formulas
entry.content:
Hi Dan,

I'm not sure if I misunderstood this, but in the post where you say "When the user taps the button, set the hashToSubmit app variable using a formula ... " would it not be the stringToHash app variable? Are we supposed to be hashing our value (eg. $123,456.99) or is that value we are supposed to use as our hash?

Thanks in advance!

BRs,

Lisa Shang

entry.title: Re: Reading data from Excel to Database tables in IDM
entry.content: Hi Arvind,

entry.title: How to trigger tag for "REWORK QUANTITY" from DM to ERP
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am trying to send rework quantity from SAP DM to ERP. As we can see in the below screenshots, there is a section in ERP for "confirmed rework" and we also have a tag for "rework" on the DM side in the payload for "Production order yield confirmation":

Screenshots:

Scenarios Tested:

  1. NC logging and moving to disposition > completing order in the rework operation
  2. NC logging and moving to disposition > completing order in the original operation
  3. NC logging and moving to disposition > completing order in the original operation + component assembly at the end
  4. Before order release in DM, assigning normal op activities and setting it as rework > then processing the order.

In the 3rd scenario the payload mentioned before was seen. But the tag for rework is still sending the value "false". 

Question: How to send the tag rework as true so that it gets reflected in ERP? Any help will be valuable. 

Thanks and Regards,
Harsh.

entry.content:
Hi,

Did you fid the solution? i also facing the same issue now please share it

 

SAPUI5  SAP Fiori Cloud

entry.title: Re: Regulatory Change Manager: Your Solution to Navigate Regulatory Changes with Ease
entry.content:
In the old Announcement of Legal Changes app it was possible to search with "On-Premise-Product" and the Component "SLL" for GTS relevant Announcements. If I try to find now in the new Regulatory Change Manager the Legal Changes for the Product "SAP Global Trade Services" there will come no entries. And it doesn't matter if I search directly for the product wihtout any other select entries or just enter "ATLAS" in the search field and don't fill in anything else. 
How to find in future with the new Regulatory Change Manager the Announcement of Legal Changes for GTS? Can someone please assist?

Thank you and best regards,

Susanne

entry.title: Re: i am doing 101k while posting GR got an error pir not found in p.org
entry.content: A littlebit more detailed information would be more helpful. However, did you check, if the PIR exists for the material and vendor in the purchasing organization specified in the Purchase Order? If yes, did you check if it is valid for the current date?

entry.title: Re: Whenever I use a custom action, it opens a new object...
entry.content: If "it opens a blank object page (uuid full of 0s)", it is definite that the result is not filled by the implementation.

entry.title: Partner-2-Partner Collaboration in Manufacturing
entry.content:
In the ever-evolving landscape of manufacturing, collaboration has become the cornerstone for success. With SAP's robust strategy focusing on cloud, platform, and an expanding partner ecosystem, the imperative for SAP reselling partners to collaborate with solution partners is more pronounced than ever.

One such exemplary collaboration unfolds between Telekom Deutschland, a leading SAP Platinum partner, and blue-zone, a German-based specialist ISV for the SAP midmarket. In this article, we delve into the intricacies of this partnership, uncovering its significance and impact on the manufacturing sector.  

Interview with Ines Marquardt, Head of SAP Cloud Partner Sales, at Telekom Deutschland 

Q:  Can you introduce your company briefly?
Answer: Telekom is known as a telecommunications company. We have long since ceased to be that; we are changing and have matured into something more, a leading global company for ICT solutions. Our ambition in Telekom Deutschland is to become the leading digital B2B telco. Our product range includes solutions for SMEs, from start-ups to listed companies. SAP and Telekom are among the most valuable German companies, and Telekom is the most recognised telecommunications brand worldwide.

Telekom offers a comprehensive end-to-end SAP portfolio which covers a full range of services, from consultation and the transformation of existing SAP systems to the integration of application management and service. Deutsche Telekom’s experienced experts focus particularly on migration to SAP S/4HANA or the cloud. With over 20 years of experience in the cloud business and a global network of more than 2,300 Telekom SAP experts of the Telekom Group, the company ensures extensive industry coverage. The new RISE with SAP Premium Suppliership by T-Systems round off our complete end-to-end portfolio.

Telekom holds the status of an SAP Platinum Partner and engages in PE Sell.

Company Size Focus: Small Enterprise (5 to 49 Employees), Medium Enterprise (50 to 249 Employees), Large Enterprise (more than 250 Employees).

Industry Focuses: Public, Professional Services, Healthcare, Retail, Telecommunications, Industrial machinery and components, Cross Industry.

Q:  How did you end up leveraging solution(s) developed by blue-zone?
A:  We have built up an SAP ecosystem with around 60 SAP partners in order to operate flexibly and in close proximity to our customers. New kid on the bloc in this eco system: blue-Zone with whom the company plans to offer SAP solutions to medium-sized businesses in the Industry Solutions sector.

The partnership between Telekom and blue-zone has its roots in the mobile telephony and ICT communications industry, where we are already working together successfully. We want to expand this good cooperation to the SAP area in order to be able to offer our customers a more comprehensive range of services. The personal meetings at Digital X (the largest German digitalization trade fair by Telekom) and SAP Connect last year laid the foundation for this expanded collaboration and to solidify our partnership.

This partnership between Telekom and blue-zone exemplifies our commitment to offering innovative and customer-centric solutions. We look forward to the opportunities this cooperation opens up and to shaping the digital future together.

Q:  What would you say are the biggest benefits of leveraging the solution(s) from blue-zone?
A:  Our main goal is to offer our customers a wider range of high-quality services while benefiting from both companies' expertise in the SAP area. Together with blue-zone, we can complement our range of SAP solutions and align them even more closely with customer needs.

Both partners are well-equipped and optimally aligned for this purpose: blue-zone contributes profound insights and a comprehensive understanding of the Industry-Solutions-specific needs, while Deutsche Telekom draws on long-standing experience of SAP transformation and an end-to-end SAP portfolio offering which spans consulting, implementation, operation, maintenance and reselling.

Q:  Why do you think blue-zone’s solutions will fit your customers?
A: We have a broad customer base and many of our customers are looking for simple solutions that are tailored to their needs. blue-zone offers these, SAP add-ons close to the standard, which provide our customers with the added value they need to gain a competitive advantage.

The focus is not only on SAP. The partnership offers new business models and innovation projects relating to future fields such as digitalization, the Internet of Things, machine-to-machine communication (M2M), Industry 4.0, artificial intelligence (AI) and sustainability, which enable SMEs in the Industry Solutions sector to tap into new efficiency and value creation potential.

blue-zone has many solutions we will leverage. But I think the solution EDI-ZONE (electronic data interchange) and DOM-ZONE (smart document output management) would be the first ones.

Q:  How do you and blue-zone collaborate in areas like sales, implementation, support etc ?
A: In this partnership, Telekom brings in the leads and acts as the general contractor in front of the customer. We leverage our market position and good customer relationships to identify and acquire projects. blue-zone then takes on the role of the expert for consulting, potential implementation, and the operation of the SAP solutions, creating a comprehensive service offering for us.

Partners play a crucial role in the transformation: they bring expertise (depending on the product and industry), they are quick, flexible, and deliver reliable quality. We have a strong SAP partner ecosystem that complements our Magenta Delivery

Interview with Tom Bajonczak, Channel Sales Manager, blue-zone Gmbh

Q: Can you introduce your company briefly?
A: blue-zone GmbH is a pure software vendor (ISV) with a clear focus on innovative, cloud-based solutions. Our focus is on product lifecycle management, technological expertise and the development of scalable and consistent standard products with the highest quality control. We support our partners in concentrating their resources on their core business by relieving them of the burden of customer-specific developments. Our goal is for our partners to successfully win and implement customer projects while building long-term customer loyalty.

Q: How do you look at the relationship with Telekom?
A: The partnership promises numerous benefits and synergies by combining the strengths of both companies. The aim is a win-win situation with long-term growth, innovation and added value. For blue-zone, the partnership means scaling opportunities and market expansion through access to Telekom's resources and customers. In addition, Telekom covers almost all areas of digitalization for its customers. Telekom has been relying on specialized partners for years to address all topics with the necessary expertise. It is impressive how a company of this size is able to form partnerships at eye level with partners of all sizes. The collaboration enables faster digitalization and agile solutions. Both companies can learn from each other, share best practices and drive innovation together, based on trust, respect and mutual benefit.

Q: Do you look for more resellers of your solution(s)?
A: As a software vendor (ISV), it is crucial to gain additional partners in order to bring our product to new markets and expand our reach. Through strategic partnerships, we can leverage synergies to improve our technology, better understand customer needs and offer innovative solutions. New partners bring fresh perspectives and resources that can accelerate our growth. In addition, they allow us to expand our product portfolio and leverage specific expertise in different industries. Through a diverse partner landscape, we can strengthen our competitiveness and ensure long-term success for all three parties.

Conclusion
Both blue-zone and Telekom are SAP partners taking advantage of SAPs cloud platform strategy to further enhance their businesses.  And together with SAP they help customers even more in a win-win-win-win proposition.

Are you an organization that think you can benefit from the expertise of these SAP partners?  Consider contacting them to explore possibilities. 

Are you a SAP reseller which think you and your customers could benefit from the solutions developed by blue-zone, or do you think it would be interesting to collaboration with Deutsche Telekom?  Reach out to these organisations and discuss business opportunities.

Blue-zone solutions on SAP Store
Deutsche Telekom Geschäftskunden
blue-zone

entry.title: Re: BOM indicator
entry.content:
As I know, there isn't a direct BOM indicator in the MM03 transaction, but you should still find out if a material has a BOM or if it's part of a BOM using other standard transactions. Did you try to use the CS03 transaction? You just need to enter the material number and plant. If the material has a BOM, it will be displayed. To check if a material is part of a BOM, you can use the CS15 transaction.

For mass checking, may you can try to use the SE16N transaction with the tables STKO (for BOM headers) and STPO (for BOM items). You can filter by material number to find all BOMs related to a specific material.

entry.title: UI5 Smarttable barcode
entry.content:
Hi,

Is there a way to show a barcode in a smarttable where fields have been defined with annotations ?.

Is it on onBeforeRebindTable event where I need to convert the smarttable cell's value to a barcode using a formatter?.

Thanks,

Javier

entry.title: How to disable multiple logging from one User id in sap public cloud?
Re: I have a confusion regarding SAP Community ID
entry.content: yes that is the id you have to paste in the google form

entry.title: Re: Advance Intercompany sales Purchase order Posting Error
entry.content: It seems like you are facing an issue with the configuration of transit plants for different company codes. For your first scenario, if you need to activate the intercompany scenario for different plants in Company code BMDE & BMGB, you may need to maintain separate entries for each plant in the configuration table V_T001_TRNSTPLNT. This table is used to define the transit plant for each company code and sales organization. For your second scenario, if you have multiple transit plants in one single company code, you may need to maintain separate entries for each transit plant in the configuration table V_T001_TRNSTPLNT. This should allow you to define the correct transit plant for each sales organization and company code. May you can give them a try and check, if this works..

entry.title: Custom CDS view unable to add items due to not release
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

In View browser app, some items are in not released state

  • C_PurchaseReqnWorkItem
  • C_WorkItemPurReqnHdrDetail
  • C_WorkItemPurReqnHdrItemDetail
  • C_WorkItemPurReqnItemDetail

So, we unable to add this items in Custom CDS App. Attached document for your reference.

Can you please release the items in our system to develop the new report?

 

Thanks & Regards,

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Custom CDS view unable to add items due to not release
entry.content:
Hello,

Kindly refer to the below Link:

Solved: Unreleased CDS Views - SAP Community

 

Best Regards,

SAP SUPPORT

entry.title: Re: How do I stop getting duplicate records when I use on...
entry.content:
Hi Mike,

When you add the position title or code from Job Info you are using an auto-join with the position table. A position can have multiple record statuses and will return a row for each status. I recommend joining the position table from Job Info and create a query filter on Position ? Record Status = "N". This will give you one row only. See an example below.

Kind regards,

Jeroen

entry.title: Re: What's New for SAP Build Apps - Enhanced Fusion Development
entry.content:
Hi @EsmeeX 

Thanks for the blog, it's really nice and helpful!
It would be nice to add what kind of errors will be return if this configurations wasn't done or wasn't done correctly. In this way people could find this blog when searching for their error.

for example , see this community issue
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sap-build-app-after-deployed-into-btp-getting-error-while-open-app-error/qaq-p/13656471

BR

entry.title: Re: MTO item Category in Sales Inquiry
entry.content: Did you check the Item Category Determination? SPRO -> Sales and Distribution -> Sales -> Sales Documents -> Sales Document Item -> Assign Item Categories. Here, check if the item category determination is set up correctly for the combination of sales document type, item category group, usage and higher level item category.

entry.title: Your Essential Toolset for Planning & Migrating to SAP S/4HANA - 2024 Edition
entry.content:
SAP’s Ranjeet Panicker's presentation, "Your Essential Toolset for Planning & Migrating to SAP S/4HANA - 2024 Edition," offers a comprehensive guide to navigating the complexities of transitioning to SAP S/4HANA. Emphasizing a hands-on, interactive approach, Ranjeet showcases the importance of engaging teams through simulations and games, highlighting the system's capabilities in a practical context.

Key recommendations include conducting early readiness checks, leveraging SAP Signavio for process transformation, and developing solid business cases to ensure stakeholder buy-in. The talk also underscores the significance of managing custom code, utilizing the SAP Roadmap Viewer for strategic planning, and the value of community engagement for continuous learning. With a focus on optimization and transformation, Ranjeet provides actionable insights for organizations aiming to harness the full potential of SAP S/4HANA, making the session an invaluable resource for enterprises embarking on their migration journey.

Key Takeaways:

Turn planning into action with interactive tools and simulations.

  • Engage teams through simulation games to experience SAP S/4HANA capabilities firsthand.
  • Use fictional business scenarios to demonstrate the system's real-world applications and benefits.

Start your SAP S/4HANA journey with a readiness check — know where you stand to plan where you're going.

  • Run SAP S/4HANA readiness checks early to assess system and custom code readiness.
  • Use the insights from readiness checks to inform your migration strategy and identify areas needing attention.

Optimize, don't just migrate — transform your business processes for a future-ready enterprise.

  • Leverage SAP Signavio to analyze and optimize current business processes.
  • Identify process improvements and efficiencies as a foundational step in your migration to SAP S/4HANA.

Your roadmap to SAP S/4HANA is not just about technology; it's about creating value at every step.

  • Develop clear business cases highlighting the value and benefits of the migration.
  • Ensure every phase of your migration strategy is aligned with business objectives and outcomes.

Leverage the community and continuous learning to navigate the SAP S/4HANA migration landscape.

  • Engage with the SAP community and peers to share experiences and best practices.
  • Foster an environment of continuous learning to keep abreast of the latest SAP S/4HANA developments and innovations.

Themes

  • Tool Time Theme: Ranjeet leverages the concept of "Tool Time" to make the session interactive and engaging, reminiscent of the TV show where tools and their applications are demonstrated. This approach highlights the practical aspects of SAP S/4HANA migration tools.

 

Recommendations

  • Leverage Interactive Tools for Engagement: Ranjeet suggests using interactive simulations and games to engage teams in understanding the capabilities of SAP S/4HANA, such as simulation games that pit teams against each other in a fictional business scenario to experience system functionalities firsthand.

 

  • Comprehensive Planning Is Key: Emphasize thorough planning phases, including strategic considerations for custom code management, process optimization, and transformation to ensure a smooth transition to SAP S/4HANA.

 

  • Utilize SAP Signavio for Process Transformation: To optimize and transform business processes, the use of SAP Signavio is recommended. This tool helps in analyzing existing processes and identifying areas for improvement.

 

  • Run Readiness Checks Early and Often: Ranjeet advises running SAP S/4HANA readiness checks well in advance of the planned migration date. These checks provide valuable insights into the system's readiness, custom code analysis, and potential impact areas.

 

  • Manage Custom Code Efficiently: It's important to analyze and manage custom code by identifying what is necessary, optimizing it for the new environment, and possibly retiring unused or redundant code.

 

  • Focus on Value Cases and Business Case Development: Develop solid business cases that clearly outline the value and benefits of migrating to SAP S/4HANA, addressing any business concerns and ensuring stakeholder buy-in.

 

  • Explore the SAP Roadmap Viewer: Use the SAP Roadmap Viewer as a guide through the different phases of the project, from discovery to deployment. This tool provides a comprehensive view of necessary activities, best practices, and available resources for each phase.

 

  • Seek Peer Experiences and Customer Stories: For insights and validation, look into customer stories and experiences of similar organizations that have undergone the migration process. This can help in setting realistic expectations and learning from best practices.

 

  • Embrace Continuous Learning and Community Engagement: Engage with the SAP community and peers to share experiences, challenges, and solutions. Continuous learning and collaboration can provide support and insights throughout the migration process.

 

  • Consider Downtime Optimization Techniques: For organizations concerned about downtime during the transition, explore tools and techniques like Downtime Optimized DMO and Near Zero Downtime Technology (nZDT) to minimize system unavailability.

 

Remember:

Access Presentation: Here         

Watch the YouTube Video of Ranjeet's Presentation with demos: Here  

 

CTA
For Enterprise Architect SAP Community Updates and More: Connect with and Follow Paul Kurchina - https://www.linkedin.com/in/paulkurchina/

 

Flag Photo with Ranjeet

entry.title: Re: Backup Server India
entry.content: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/302810 Appears to be no longer accessible in that board and without indication of what action/response was provided from the SAP team.

entry.title: Re: How do I stop getting duplicate records when I use on...
entry.content: 3211995 - Duplicate records in Story report due to selection of Foundation object fields - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: AO 2.8 SP20 vs AO 2.8 SP21
entry.content:
Hello @2023_kurt,

AO 2.8 SP21 is available in both versions - "SAP Analysis for Office Standard Edition" and "SAP Analysis for Office Edition for SAP Analytics Cloud". Both need a local installation and can't be used in Excel Online. The differences between these two editions are listed in the PAM for Analysis for Office. Scheduling is only possible with Standard Edition within the BI Platform (see page 6 of the linked document).

"SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft Office" is a cloud based tool and doesn't need a local installation. It works fine in Excel Online, too. You can use both (SAP Analysis for Office and the Add-in) in parallel:

The tabs "Analysis" and "Analysis Design" belong to SAP Analysis for Office. The tab "SAP Analytics Cloud" belongs to the Add-in. The Add-in is the future but has a lot of lacks compared to SAP Analysis for Office. I hope the gaps will be closed in the future.

Best regards

Lothar

entry.title: What is now the recommended picture size for item icons for the new learning experience?
entry.content:
If I look at the images page for learning administration it still recommends to use the 180x180px images. But if you do that, you get 2 bars on your item tiles on the integrated learning experience page.

If you use a 6:9 image the image seems a bit cropped in the library view, so I'm wondering what the current guidelines are to make the images appear nice in both the libraries as on the new learning experience home page tiles.

Thank you for the updated feedback! 

Kind regards

Jan

entry.title: Re: Create a readonly role for SAP Hana Cloud HDI container
entry.content:
Did you try in SAP HANA Cloud Cockpit with your DBADMIN account in "Security" section to create a new role by adding "_SYS_BI_CP_ALL" role?

This role should provides read-only access to all objects in the system.

In the "Object Privileges" section, click on "Add" and select the HDI container schema that you want to grant read-only access to. Set the "Privilege" to "SELECT", save it and create the role. If this happen, you need to assign this role to a user. Select the user you want to assign the role to, and click on "Edit", in the "Granted Roles" section, click on "Add" and select the "ReadOnlyRole" you just created. Save it and assign the role to the user. Now, the user should have read-only access to the HDI container.

May you give them a try to check, if this is working for you.

entry.title: Re: April (Citizen) Developer Challenge – SAP Build Apps: Task 2 - Formulas
entry.content:
Fixed.

Thanks for finding the error – I'd like to say it was meant to keep you on your toes but, alas, it was just my mistake 😸

Thx

entry.title: I have also facing the same issue, can any one suggest how to reprocess leave request in sap
entry.content: Can any one help how to update leave request in 2001 info type

entry.title: Re: how to clean up the change lists after many year usage of HALM?
entry.content: Did you try to using the archiving functionality in HALM? This will move the change lists to an archive area where they can still be accessed if needed, but they will not be included in the main list of change lists.

entry.title: Re: Quote 2.0 : Displaying a message to the User in a spe...
entry.content:
Hi Fancy,

The goal is to display a message for a certain custom quote table when a button is clicked. Do you maybe know how i can proceed it ?! 

Thanks a lot 

Best regards 

Lola

entry.title: Re: Story Report Calculated Column finding the MAX
entry.content:
Hi maseitner,

I recommend using a crosstab table in your story. You can then create a calculation of type aggregation and use the MAX function. This should work.

Kind regards,

Jeroen

entry.title: Re: RICEFW Concept
entry.content: Hi SAP Experts, Have joined the group to explore more.

entry.title: Re: BTP trial Account cannot access BAS
entry.content: Using the incognito mode also works for a subaccount on a global BTP account. I wasn't able to open the Dev Space Manager (due to the message "Access denied") after adding the SAP Business Application Studio Service to my subaccount and assigning the necessary roles for the users. But accessing the BTP Cockpit in incognito mode and opening the Dev Space Manager from there worked for me! Thank you. 🙂

entry.title: Re: way to report a no show after 30 days have passed tha...
entry.content:
Hello information from SAP is the following:

  • Go to Import Employee Data.
  • Download the template of Termination Details.
  • Fill all "No show" required fields (such as: end-date, event-reason, person-id-external, user-id, okToRehire)
  • Import it back to "Termination Details".

entry.title: Re: way to report a no show after 30 days have passed tha...
entry.content:
Hello Information from SAP is the following:

  • Go to Import Employee Data.
  • Download the template of Termination Details.
  • Fill all "No show" required fields (such as: end-date, event-reason, person-id-external, user-id, okToRehire)
  • Import it back to "Termination Details"
